question.text: Which options generally have the HIGHEST vega (most sensitive to volatility changes)?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Vega is highest for: 1. LONGER-DATED options (more time for volatility to impact the price) 2. ATM options (most sensitive to uncertainty)
Longer-dated ATM options have the maximum vega because they have the most time value at stake and are most affected by volatility changes.
**The Trap:** Shorter-dated options have LOWER vega. Deep ITM/OTM have lower vega than ATM.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Contrast with theta which is highest for short-dated ATM options.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Highest vega: long-dated ATM. Vega decreases with less time and away from ATM.
